title: Mamata storms EVM strongroom; TMC, BJP lock horns over tampering claims: How late-night drama unfolded
content: The Trinamool Congress staged a protest in Kolkata, alleging EVM tampering and procedural lapses in strongrooms before the West Bengal assembly polls. The party accused the BJP and Election Commission of collusion, while the EC asserted the strongrooms were secure and footage showed routine postal ballot segregation.

title: 'Continue seeing Gaza on YouTube': Netanyahu mocks flotilla as 20 ships with 175 activists sent back
content: Israel's Navy successfully intercepted a Gaza-bound flotilla carrying activists near Crete, preventing any vessels from reaching Palestinian shores. Prime Minister Netanyahu commended the operation, stating all participants were turned back. The US State Department backed Israel's actions, calling the flotilla a counterproductive stunt organized by a pro-Hamas entity.

title: Watch: Terrifying moment as cruise boat flips over at Jabalpur dam mid-ride; 9 dead
content: A cruise boat capsized at Jabalpur's Bargi Dam on Thursday evening. The vessel was caught in a sudden storm with high winds. Nine people have died, and 22 were rescued. Officials are investigating the incident. The state government announced compensation for the families of the deceased.

title: Explained: Was Jason Holder's catch fair in Rajat Patidar dismissal?
content: A controversial catch by Jason Holder dismissed Rajat Patidar during the RCB vs GT IPL clash, sparking outrage. Despite replays appearing inconclusive, the third umpire upheld the decision. Former spinner Ravichandran Ashwin clarified that Holder's grip likely prevented the ball from touching the ground, validating the umpire's call.

title: JPMorgan executive accused of sexually assaulting 'brown boy Indian'
content: A JPMorgan Chase executive is accused in a lawsuit of sexually harassing and abusing a junior male employee, including allegations of drugging him and racial abuse. The plaintiff claims the firm retaliated after he reported the conduct, placing him on involuntary leave. JPMorgan Chase denies the allegations, stating an internal investigation found no evidence.

title: American billionaire Mark Cuban to engineers: Use these 3 Claude prompts to sharpen your skills
content: Mark Cuban urges engineers to master AI, particularly by practicing with Claude. He suggests three prompts to build practical business agents, highlighting opportunities for young professionals to implement AI for cost savings and efficiency. Despite AI's rapid disruption and challenges like cost and reliability, Cuban emphasizes adaptation for competitive advantage.

title: 'He's a lowlife, always has been': Trump ramps up attack on Jimmy Kimmel
content: President Trump has escalated his feud with Jimmy Kimmel, calling the late-night host a "lowlife" and suggesting ABC's broadcast license is in "great jeopardy." Trump criticized Kimmel's jokes about Melania Trump and his criticism of Republicans, demanding his firing.

title: New US visa rule: Effective immediately, applicants must affirm no fear of returning home
content: America's Trump administration has introduced a new visa rule. Diplomats will now question visa applicants about fearing their home country. This aims to curb asylum claims through the visa system.  Applicants must deny fearing harm to proceed.  This affects tourist, student, and business visas.  Immigration experts express concerns about vulnerable individuals.

title: India's World Cup stars needed a break before IPL 2026: Ian Bishop
content: Former West Indies pacer Ian Bishop suggests India's T20 World Cup winners might have benefited from a longer break before the IPL, citing their subsequent inconsistent performances. He also advocates for more varied pitches to balance the game between bat and ball, noting that strong bowling is crucial for champion teams.

title: 'Son found out about her affair': Hyderabad techie jumps to death in lake, wife arrested for abetment
content: Bachupally police have apprehended a woman and her alleged lover for abetting the suicide of her husband, a techie. The deceased reportedly took his life after discovering his wife's affair and the alleged online sharing of private videos. The investigation is ongoing following the family's complaint.

title: Dhurandhar 2 box office day 43: Ranveer Singh starrer nears Baahubali 2 global mark
content: Ranveer Singh's latest release, 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge', is generating immense buzz as it edges closer to 'Baahubali 2's global box office achievement. Over the past six weeks, this espionage thriller has earned an impressive Rs 1783.66 crore globally, and with Maharashtra showing particular enthusiasm, it might just outstrip the Rs 1,788.06 crore milestone by this weekend. Read on to know more in detail.

title: Infosys CEO on AI impacting jobs: My own sense is AI is going to create a lot more opportunity
content: Infosys CEO Salil Parekh believes AI will reshape India's IT pyramid model, creating more opportunities than job losses. The company is adapting hiring and training to integrate AI, seeing a $300 billion market. While the shift to a specialized talent structure will be gradual, Infosys is encouraging AI token usage to drive client growth and efficiency.

title: Under US Congress deadline pressure, Trump admin claims Iran ceasefire 'terminated' hostilities
content: The Trump administration asserts the U.S. is not at war with Iran, despite approaching the War Powers Resolution's 60-day deadline. House Speaker Mike Johnson echoed this, stating no congressional authorization is needed as active hostilities have ceased. This stance, however, faces challenges from Democrats who argue the deadline remains binding.

title: 'Cricket wants to go football way, purists not ready': Heinrich Klaasen
content: South African batter Heinrich Klaasen suggests T20 cricket should be treated as a distinct sport, allowing leagues to thrive and globalize the game. He believes cricketers are moving towards football-like careers, though purists resist a future where international cricket is diminished. Klaasen emphasizes T20's role in evolving the sport and coaching methodologies.

title: 'Life jackets given after boat started sinking': What led to Jabalpur tragedy that killed 9
content: A cruise boat operated by the Madhya Pradesh tourism department capsized in Bargi Dam near Jabalpur, resulting in at least nine deaths. The vessel, carrying 43 passengers, overturned during a sudden, severe storm with high winds. Survivors allege life jackets were distributed only after the boat began sinking, raising questions about safety protocols.

title: Dianna Russini scandal: New allegations emerge amid Mike Vrabel controversy
content: Controversy around Dianna Russini has intensified after unverified allegations linked her to multiple coaches following viral photos with Mike Vrabel. An alleged former scout’s claim added fuel, though no evidence supports it. Russini’s exit from The Athletic and social media has raised questions. Meanwhile, legal expert Michael McCann noted potential privacy issues surrounding how the photos were obtained.

title: D4vd case update: Celeste Rivas’ death certificate lists pregnancy status unknown
content: Celeste Rivas Hernandez's death certificate has been updated, now stating her pregnancy status in the year prior to her death is unknown, a change from previous reports. The document also confirms her cause of death as "multiple penetrating injuries" and manner as "homicide," with other details like the exact injury circumstances now listed as unknown.

title: Candace Owens denies murder accusation claims amid Andrew Kolvet chat leak
content: Candace Owens faced fresh online backlash after a strange Charlie Kirk show clip and a leaked chat screenshot went viral. A controversial line from the chat sparked strong reactions, but Owens said it was taken out of context and meant as a joke. The debate now centers on authenticity, editing, and whether the full story is being shown.

title: US tightens visa rules, requires applicants to declare no persecution concerns in home country
content: US embassies now require nonimmigrant visa applicants to declare they don't fear persecution back home. This new rule, aimed at preventing asylum claims upon arrival, asks applicants if they've faced harm or fear it upon return. Experts warn this could force individuals into dangerous migration routes, jeopardizing their safety and families.

title: Windfall gains tax cut: Excise duty on diesel exports down to Rs 23/litre, ATF exports to Rs 33/litre
content: In a statement, the Finance Ministry also announced that the road and infrastructure cess on diesel exports will be waived for the next fortnight starting May 1. Meanwhile, the export duty on petrol will continue to remain at zero.

title: 'Don't want to look 20 pounds heavier': Trump on wearing bulletproof vest during dinner attack
content: President Trump dismissed reports of wearing a bulletproof vest, joking about the added weight. He acknowledged security considerations but expressed reluctance to appear overly protected. Trump stated he avoids dwelling on personal security threats to remain effective in his duties, following an attempted assassination at a White House event.

title: 3-layer security grid in place: 1,500 personnel guard Kolkata strong rooms ahead of counting day
content: Kolkata's 22 strong rooms are under a robust three-layered security system involving CAPF and Kolkata Police, with 1,500 personnel on duty per shift. Across the state, over 200 CAPF companies and 7,500 Bengal Police personnel are safeguarding 294 strong rooms and 77 counting centres. Strict protocols are in place to prevent unauthorized access and maintain peace on counting day.

title: Iran to play in 2026 FIFA World Cup, confirms FIFA president Gianni Infantino
content: FIFA President Gianni Infantino has confirmed Iran's participation in the 2026 World Cup, set to be held in the United States, Mexico, and Canada.  Despite recent geopolitical tensions and reports of visa issues for Iranian officials, Infantino stressed football's power to unite a divided world, assuring Iran will play in the USA.

title: Virat Kohli loses cool, confronts umpire after controversial Patidar dismissal
content: Virat Kohli was seen in an animated exchange with an umpire during the Gujarat Titans vs. Royal Challengers Bengaluru IPL match. The incident occurred after Rajat Patidar's dismissal, with replays appearing inconclusive. Despite the controversy, the third umpire upheld the on-field decision, leaving Kohli and the RCB dugout visibly frustrated.

title: 11cm in 1 hour: Deluge could have filled 30,000 pools, two-thirds of Bengaluru’s lakes
content: Bengaluru experienced its wettest April day on Wednesday, with over 11cm of rain, equivalent to nearly 10% of its annual rainfall. This deluge, estimated at 20-22 million cubic metres, could have met the city's water needs for a fortnight.

title: Watch: New footage of shooter Cole Allen casing hotel before White House dinner attack released
content: Federal prosecutors released surveillance video showing Cole Tomas Allen attempting to storm the White House Correspondents' Dinner, armed with guns and knives, to assassinate Donald Trump. The footage depicts Allen breaching a Secret Service checkpoint at the Washington Hilton Hotel. He exchanged gunfire with agents, injuring one officer, before being apprehended.

title: ‘Don’t count Vaibhav Sooryavanshi’s age’: Dhawan’s bold India debut statement
content: Shikhar Dhawan champions young Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, emphasizing performance over age. Dhawan lauded the teenager's fearless batting against top bowlers, a rarity at his age. Sooryavanshi's impressive IPL 2026 stats, including 400 runs at a 238.10 strike rate and 37 sixes, back Dhawan's praise. However, Dhawan acknowledges the challenge of breaking into India's strong top order.

title: 'Why shouldn’t I?': Trump signals troop cuts in Italy, Spain over Iran conflict
content: President Trump is considering reducing US troop presence in Italy and Spain, citing their lack of support for the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz efforts. This move signals a widening rift with European allies over military objectives and burden-sharing within Nato.

title: Britney Spears charged with driving under the influence of alcohol and drugs
content: Pop icon Britney Spears faces charges in California for driving under the influence of drugs and alcohol. The 44-year-old star was arrested in March after being pulled over for erratic driving. She has since entered a substance abuse treatment facility. Spears' arraignment is scheduled for Monday, though her appearance is not mandatory for this misdemeanor charge.

title: MP: Kanha tigress, all 4 cubs die in suspected canine distemper virus outbreak
content: A tigress and her last cub have died at Kanha Tiger Reserve, bringing the state's tiger death toll to 30 this year. Officials suspect canine distemper virus (CDV) as the cause, potentially spread from domestic dogs. A large-scale sanitization and vaccination operation is underway to prevent further transmission.

title: Move over taco, nacho takes over: Trump earns new nickname amid Hormuz blockade
content: Market analysts have shifted from the 'TACO' nickname for President Trump to 'NACHO' amid escalating tensions over the Strait of Hormuz. This new label reflects growing skepticism that the crucial global energy route will reopen soon, despite ongoing diplomatic efforts and trade disputes.

title: 'Time to say goodbye' :Epstein's suicide note remains sealed by court
content: A recently surfaced report reveals Jeffrey Epstein penned a note before his 2019 death in a New York federal jail. Discovered by his cellmate, Nicholas Tartaglione, the handwritten message, now sealed by a judge, reportedly asserted Epstein's innocence and included the poignant phrase, "Time to say goodbye.

title: Woman accuses Andhra cop of sexual assault, issuing death threats to her
content: A woman has accused Circle Inspector Chinna Mallaiah of sexual assault and death threats after he allegedly entered her home while she was alone. The incident occurred in Vinukonda rural mandal, Palnadu district, prompting an investigation and medical examination of the victim. The officer, currently in vacancy reserve, has faced previous allegations of threatening political leaders.

title: Millions of graphene toothbrushes sold worldwide: Scientists reveal why people are buying them
content: New research from KAIST has clarified how graphene oxide's antibacterial properties work, revealing it selectively attacks harmful bacteria by targeting a specific lipid found in their membranes. This mechanism explains the success of graphene-based hygiene products and offers a novel approach to combating antibiotic resistance by physically disrupting bacterial structures.

title: To foil West Bengal post-poll unrest, EC to retain 70,000 CAPF personnel
content: Ahead of West Bengal's vote counting, the Election Commission is deploying approximately 70,000 central forces to maintain order, especially in areas prone to post-poll violence. Control rooms will monitor counting day, May 4, for immediate action against any disturbances. Strict legal action awaits those involved in violence, aiming to prevent a repeat of the 2021 incidents.

title: Cruise boat capsize: Tragedy exposes safety gaps, mirrors past lapses in Bhopal
content: A recent cruise boat capsizing in Jabalpur's Bargi dam has sparked outrage over Madhya Pradesh Tourism's alleged safety negligence.  Eyewitnesses reported a lack of life jackets and inadequate safety measures, echoing a similar near-disaster in Bhopal four years ago. Despite promises of reforms, inspections remain irregular, leaving tourists demanding accountability and stricter enforcement.

title: ‘Manipulation taking place’: Mamata raises EVM concerns, vows ‘life and death’ fight
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee visited a strong room, alleging EVM manipulation and obstruction by Central forces. She vowed to fight "life and death" against alleged bias by the Election Commission, despite exit polls favouring the BJP. The state saw record voter turnout amidst political tensions.

title: IPL 2026, RR vs DC: Will Mitchell Starc play today? Munaf Patel drops big hint
content: Delhi Capitals receive a significant boost with Mitchell Starc's availability for their IPL 2026 clash against Rajasthan Royals. Bowling coach Munaf Patel confirmed the pacer's recovery, highlighting his crucial role in a struggling attack. Despite recent batting collapses, Patel expressed confidence in the team's approach, emphasising a do-or-die mindset for the upcoming must-win game.

title: 75+ Labour Day 2026 wishes, messages & quotes to celebrate the spirit of hard work
content: Labour Day, observed on May 1st, honours the dedication of workers across all professions. This global celebration acknowledges the collective efforts that drive societal progress. While not universally a public holiday, many Indian states observe it, with government offices and banks typically closed.

title: Sri Lanka U-19 cricketers arrested for allegedly filming women in hotel bathrooms
content: Two Sri Lanka Under-19 cricketers were arrested in Colombo after women accused them of filming them while bathing in a hotel. The players, released on bail, are under police investigation for potential online sharing of alleged videos. Sri Lanka Cricket has yet to announce disciplinary measures amidst recent board changes.

title: 13-year-old Goa girl dies during training after hockey ball hits her on head
content: A 13-year-old student, Rueisha Kharsel, tragically died after being struck by a hockey ball during a school training session in Guirim. The incident occurred on the last day of school before summer vacations. She was rushed to the hospital but was declared brought dead, and an unnatural death case has been registered.

title: From school to PG, girls now outnumber boys
content: India witnesses a significant shift as girls now lead in education, outperforming boys in school enrolment and higher education pass-outs. While the overall literacy gap persists, younger generations show near parity. Women dominate fields like MPhil and arts, though men still lead in STEM, indicating ongoing gendered career paths.

title: City Nature Challenge 2026: Andhra, Telangana record surge
content: A four-day global citizen science effort where cities document urban biodiversity, building valuable data through public participation

title: Paperless land registration system launched in Chamarajanagar in Karnataka
content: Under the earlier paper-based system, physical documents were prone to forgery, alterations, page replacements, and post-registration modifications. Verification processes were largely manual, increasing the chances of errors

title: BDA unearths land fraud; FIR against four, including former Revenue Department officer
content: The property transaction dates back to 2017 and allegedly involves a former Assistant Commissioner of Bengaluru sub-division who was arrested by the Special Investigation Team probing the IMA scam

title: Bargi dam cruise boat tragedy: Death toll rises to nine; search operations continue
content: A cruise boat with 29 passengers and two crew members onboard overturned in the Bargi Dam after encountering a sudden storm

title: Slithering fear: snakebite incidents trigger panic in Kerala
content: Increased instances of snakebites and resultant deaths have spread panic in Kerala. Sarath Babu George speaks to members of the bereaved families and herpetologists and other experts to understand the magnitude of the situation

title: Lift abortion time limit for minor rape survivors: Supreme Court
content: Says State and doctors cannot make such decisions for minor rape survivors; CJI calls for penal law amendment to ensure trials in such cases completed within a week, accused’s property transferred to victim

title: Unmindful of nature’s vagaries, their toil for survival continues
content: Climate change and rising temperatures are a global concern, and environmental experts are calling for urgent measures to address this crisis. However, for informal workers, changing temperatures hardly matter, as they cannot afford to miss even a single day of work. Despite the intense conditions, they continue working with few or no protective measures, explains Nellore Sravani

title: Hike in commercial LPG price: Shock and resignation on food street as LPG price goes up by 42%
content: The food kiosk is among the dozens of shacks and mulgis in the Yellareddyguda, Ameerpet and Sri Nagar Colony area with rows of hostels and PG accommodations where gig-economy workers, IT students and informal economy employees live

title: India stays on USTR priority watch list on IPR
content: “India maintains high customs duties directed to IP-intensive products such as information and communications technology (ICT) products, solar energy equipment, medical devices, pharmaceuticals, and capital goods”

title: Centre notifies changes to Citizenship Rules; focus on OCI registration
content: The changes, known as the Citizenship (Amendment) Rules, 2026, introduce a slew of online initiatives for OCI cardholders, including a digital application and renunciation process

title: Maharashtra Legislative Council elections to be unopposed as Congress backtracks, Shinde fields two candidates
content: The Congress had said it would contest the polls, however, appears to have relented after a call between the party’s Maharashtra in-charge Ramesh Chennithala and Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray

title: West Bengal weather: Rain in State bring respite from heat; more showers predicted
content: Due to favourable wind pattern and strong moisture incursion from the Bay of Bengal, thunderstorms are very likely to occur over most districts of West Bengal, IMD said

title: The siege of KBR National Park: Concrete closing in on Hyderabad’s green heart
content: KBR National Park is at the centre of fresh public outrage, this time over the flyovers and underpasses rising around it under the Hyderabad City Innovative and Transformative Infrastructure project. This island of tranquillity in the heart of a bustling metropolis has faced threats since its inception, raising persistent questions over its very status as a national park. Swathi Vadlamudi explores the existential crisis confronting the urban haven

title: New Corporations, same old problems
content: It has been eight months since the five corporations came into being in Bengaluru, in place of one large civic body. The number of wards have almost doubled from 198 to 369, expected to take governance closer to citizens. But has it really been the case, finds out Chetan B.C.

title: ‘Systemic failures’ made tribal man take his sister’s skeletal remains to bank, finds probe in Odisha
content: Revenue Divisional Commissioner, who is heading a State government probe, said there was delay in issuing death certificate to the deceased’s family

title: Mumbai Pune Missing Link Project opens on May 1. Why is it significant?
content: What is the hype around the reduction of a distance of six kilometres on India’s first expressway? What is the significance of the reduction of travel time by 20-30 minutes on a regular day?

title: Rajasthan’s emergence as innovation-led State highlighted in ‘i-Start’ review
content: Chief Secretary V. Srinivas conducted a detailed review of the activities undertaken as part of the Service Management with Artificial Intelligence and Real Time System

title: Empty LNG ship from India heads into Strait of Hormuz for loading in UAE’s Das Island
content: Umm Al Ashtan is among the first instance since the war started that an empty ship in ballast condition is sailing upstream of the Strait of Hormuz

title: ECI introduces new QR code-based ID cards for counting centres
content: Poll body launches new photo identity cards with QR codes for persons authorised to enter counting halls, including officials and candidates

title: Former Air Chief says Greater Nicobar project important for ‘military footprint’
content: In pushback against Rahul Gandhi’s opposition to the project, retired Air Chief Marshal says ecological concerns will be mitigated; notes that proposed port is 150 km from Malacca Strait, the route for 75% of China’s energy trade

title: Italy told to avoid sharing of defence tech with Pakistan
content: Talks with Italian Defence Minister focus on exclusive technologies, India-Italy cooperation and security concerns

title: Mamata visits Bhabanipur EVM strong room, warns against counting irregularities
content: The Election Commission has dismissed the claim regarding the handling of polled materials at the centre, stating that poll officials were engaged in the task of segregating postal ballots as per due process and the strongrooms remained secure

title: Tourists flock to Munnar to escape soaring temperatures
content: The influx of tourists has led to significant traffic congestion in the town, raising concerns about potential gridlock during the busy weekend ahead.

title: Union Minister stalling CBI probe into ex-INLD Haryana chief’s murder, alleges family
content: Sheila Rathee, State executive member of the INLD, says she is ‘not satisfied’ with the progress of the probe into her husband’s murder

title: India’s first green methanol plant to turn Kutch’s most invasive weed into marine fuel
content: Prosopis juliflora, which has for decades threatened biodiversity in Kutch’s Banni grasslands, will be turned into methanol; the global shipping industry is being obliged to adopt green fuels under International Maritime Organization rules

title: India halted Sindoor on its own terms: Rajnath
content: Defence Minister says Pakistan, an ‘epicentre of terrorism’, is emerging as the global hub of ‘international terrorism’; he says Indian forces have the surge capacity to scale up operations

title: Revenue-deficit States may face fiscal stress, says Centre
content: A new analysis by the Ministry of Finance said that such States would either cut productive spending or turn to the Centre for more funds

title: Chhattisgarh Assembly passes resolution for one-third quota for women in legislative bodies
content: Chief Minister Vishnu Deo Sai tabled the resolution and said that for the dignity, overall development and empowerment of women, one-third reservation for them in the Lok Sabha and all Legislative Assemblies should be enforced immediately following the delimitation exercise

title: Counselling-based transfers for sub-registrars in Karnataka to be made permanent exercise
content: Revenue Minister Krishna Byre Gowda has said Karnataka will make its counselling-based transfer system for sub-registrars a permanent, annual exercise, replacing a process that had long been viewed with suspicion

title: Data parks of combined 1,000 MW capacity to be set up in Bengaluru, Mysuru, Mangaluru
content: A preliminary meeting regarding the setting of data parks was held by Karnataka Ministers K.J. George, M.B. Patil, and Priyank Kharge

title: Tamil Nadu records highest marine fish landings in 2025
content: The State landed an estimated 6.85 lakh tonnes of fish, a slight increase from 2024 when it was 6.79 lakh tonnes, despite facing two cyclones; it surpassed Gujarat that landed 6.43 lakh tonnes

title: SEZs must diversify beyond U.S. market and IT services: CEA
content: ‘Half of SEZ exports today flow to the U.S. and the Netherlands. In a fragmenting world, that is concentration risk,’ says Anantha Nageswaran

title: Mizoram declared ‘insurgency-free’ as last militant group lays down arms
content: A total of 43 cadres of the group, including Mr. Sanate, surrendered their weapons to the government during a “homecoming and arms laying ceremony” at Sesawng near Aizawl during the day

title: Tamil Nadu records 99.9% pass in Class 10 ICSE, Class 12 ISC exams
content: A total of 3,305 students from 88 schools across Tamil Nadu appeared for the Class 12 exams. While girls achieved a pass percentage of 99.93%, 99.89% of boys passed

title: HC grants time to nine convicts in Sattankulam custodial death case to file their appeals
content: The nine policemen were sentenced to death by a trial court in Madurai for the death of trader P. Jayaraj and his son J. Benicks in Sattankulam

title: Work on Yettinahole will resume within a week, says D.K. Shivakumar
content: Shivakumar said the Centre had directed the State to pay ₹257 crore towards penalties and mitigation measures, which would be done

title: Zubeen death probe: Key accused Shyamkanu Mahanta’s bail plea rejected
content: Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma had said in the State Assembly in November last year that Garg’s death was a case of “plain and simple murder”

title: Karnataka Cabinet approves Integrated Satellite Township project in Bengaluru South district at a cost of ₹18,133 crore
content: It agrees to provide a bank guarantee to a tune of ₹7,500 crore to the project that will entail acquisition of 7,404 acres in nine villages of Ramanagara taluk in Bengaluru South district

title: Odisha Assembly: BJP, BJD and Congress resort to political one-upmanship over women’s reservation
content: Linking the amendment in Women’s Reservation Bill, which was already passed in Parliament in 2023, to the Delimitation Bill was a clandestine act to push through delimitation without discussion of facts, says BJD chief and Leader of Opposition Naveen Patnaik in special Assembly session

title: Legendary singer Runa Laila says music has a healing touch, it knows no borders
content: Runa Laila will receive the Minar-e-Dilli award at the 15th Delhi International Film Festival; she has called for greater cultural collaboration among SAARC countries

title: J&K police file case over ‘dissemination of secessionist video’
content: The police warned the general public to refrain from creating, sharing, or circulating such unlawful content

title: You can't force 15-year-old to become mom, Supreme Court tells AIIMS
content: govt must respect a citizen's autonomy of choice . a bench of CJI Surya Kant and justice . Joymalya Bagchi repeatedly . asked AIIMS to explain the pros and cons of termination of pregnancy to the parents of the 15-year-old .

title: Stock market holiday on May 1: Are NSE, BSE, MCX open on Maharashtra Day?
content: the TOI Business Desk is a vigilant and dedicated team of journalists committed to delivering the latest and most relevant business news from around the world to readers of the Times of India . the desk ensures that TOI readers are well-informed about the ever-changing and dynamic world of commerce .

title: China Building 'Super Soldiers' With Help From Harvard Scientist Convicted Of Lying To US
content: Charles Lieber, 67, was convicted of making false statements to US officials about his ties to a Chinese state program to recruit overseas talent.

title: Mamata Banerjee's Visit To Strongroom, Trinamool's Protest: Late-Night EVM Drama In Bengal
content: With just two days left for counting in the high-stakes Assembly elections, the Trinamool alleged that the ballot boxes were opened without the presence of authorised party representatives.

title: Russia's Tuapse Faces Toxic Aftermath Of Ukrainian Attacks On Refinery
content: Residents of the Russian port city of Tuapse were ordered not to drink tap water and schools remained closed on Thursday, as authorities grappled with the aftermath of the third

title: 'Fix Your Broken Country': Trump Says German Chancellor Doing "Terrible Job"
content: US President Donald Trump said the German chancellor has got "immigration and energy problems, and a problem with Ukraine."

title: US Man Executed For Killing Brother's Teen Stepdaughter Nearly 50 Years Ago
content: Convicted of first-degree murder, he was sentenced to death in 1977. Years of appeals followed and he was resentenced to death in 1988, 1993 and 1996.

title: Pete Hegseth Defends $1.5 Trillion Defence Budget Amid Iran War
content: Washington, May 1 (IANS) US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth mounted a forceful defence of President Donald Trump’s proposed $1.5 trillion military budget, arguing it reflects “the urgency of the moment” and is essential to counter a rapidly deteriorat

title: Did Trump Event Shooter Fire At Secret Service Agent? US Releases New Video
content: Jeanine Pirro, the US attorney for Washington, posted the video amid questions over whose bullet struck a Secret Service officer as the shooter ran towards the ballroom.

title: Hyderabad Techie Kills Self, Family Blames Wife's Affair, 'Mental Stress'
content: S Seetharam Reddy's father, Venkateshwar, alleged that his son was deeply disturbed by his wife's affair and other issues linked to it.

title: Aung San Suu Kyi Moved From Prison To House Arrest In Myanmar
content: Aung San Suu Kyi was detained February 1, 2021, when the army seized power from her elected government.

title: Jeffrey Epstein's Suicide Note? Ex-Cellmate Found 'Time To Say Goodbye' Letter
content: It is important to note that neither the Justice Department nor investigators into Epstein's death have examined the note so far. In fact, it hasn't even been released yet.

title: "Don't Want To Look 20 Pounds Heavier": Trump On Not Wearing A Bullet-Proof Vest
content: US President Donald Trump said Thursday he wasn't keen on wearing a bulletproof vest despite multiple alleged assassination attempts -- as he is worried it...

title: "Reserves": Pak Minister Contrasts Country's Oil Shock With India's Stability
content: Pakistan's Petroleum Minister Ali Pervaiz Malik pointed at how India is relatively stable compared to his country because of its strategic oil reserves and adequate forex reserves, which absorbed the impact of the oil disruption due to the blockade i

title: Trump Likely Among 287 Nominated For 2026 Nobel Peace Prize
content: Some 287 candidates will be considered for the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize, the secretary of the Norwegian Nobel Committee said on

title: First Direct US-Venezuela Flight In Years Arrives In Caracas
content: The first direct flight between the United States and Venezuela in seven years landed Thursday in Caracas, a new sign of thawing relations after Washington...

title: Man Visited Odisha Bank With Sister's Skeleton After He Was Told 'No' 4 Times
content: CCTV footage shows that Jitu Munda met the bank manager twice and other officials, seeking to withdraw money, before he brought his sister's body.

title: 60-Day Deadline Hours Away, Team Trump Denies Being At War With Iran
content: US Secretary of Defence Pete Hegseth also told lawmakers that he believes the ceasefire with Iran "pauses" a 60-day clock on congressional authorisation for war.
